news_headlines.title: LeBron plays 'triple threat' to spring Game 1 upset
news_headlines.description: With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves sidelined, LeBron James ignited the Lakers' offense with "a little bit of everything," posting 19 points, 13 assists, 8 rebounds and 2 steals in a Game 1 upset of the Rockets.

news_headlines.title: Buoyed by Brunson, Knicks seal 'hard-fought win'

news_headlines.title: Murray's FT dominance irks Wolves, lifts Nuggets
news_headlines.description: Jamal Murray had a tremendous night at the free throw line, making all 16 of his attempts to carry the Nuggets past the Timberwolves in Game 1, during which Nikola Jokic and Aaron Gordon had a quiet first half.

news_headlines.title: Oettinger, Stars falter, face familiar 1-0 deficit
news_headlines.description: Continuing an undesirable pattern for fans at American Airlines Center, Dallas lost Game 1 for the ninth time in the past 11 playoff series as Jake Oettinger and the Stars fell 6-1 to the Wild in a contest they quickly trailed 4-0.

tweets.text:
RT by @SharpFootball: I was on the phone with a long-time NFL exec earlier today, telling him I heard the Bengals were gonna deal the 10th pick for Dexter Lawrence. He was stunned—said it was too much, considering Lawrence needed a new deal too.

@SharpFootball with the counterpoint here 👇👇👇

RT by @SharpFootball: First intelligent comprehensive analysis I have seen. We had a saying when I was running Eagles. Best acquisition came when you got an excellent player coming off a down yr and biggest mistakes were signing an ok player coming off a good year. This analysis helps explain that.

New York’s NFL teams now control 25% of the top 16 picks in Thursday night’s draft.

The Giants are slated to pick 5th and 10th, the Jets 2nd and 16th.

This is the first time a player has been traded without any additional compensation for a haul that included a Top-10 pick in that year's draft since the Vikings traded Randy Moss to the Raiders in 2005 in exchange for a haul that included the 7th overall pick that year, per ESPN research.

A Bengals trade for Dexter Lawrence is unprecedented: This is the first time in the common draft era that dates back to 1966 in which the Bengals ever have traded a Top-10 pick for a player, per ESPN research.

More about the Giants agreeing to trade Dexter Lawrence to the Bengals in exchange for the 10th overall pick, via @Ben_Baby:

https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/48526634/sources-giants-trade-dt-lawrence-bengals-no-10-pick

RT by @AdamSchefter: Absent a trade back info the first round, this will mark just the second time in franchise history the Bengals will not make a first round pick.

The only other instance was 1989.

RT by @AdamSchefter: Giants GM Joe Schoen was in Arizona  Thursday night and dined with Arizona State WR Jordyn Tyson at the restaurant UCHI. 

5th overall may have been a little rich. 

But 10th? 🤔

Draft is five days away. Let's go.

Trade comp, via @JordanRaanan: the Bengals will receive DT Dexter Lawrence in exchange for Thursday night’s 10th overall pick, straight up. Lawrence still needs to pass his physical and finalize a contract with Cincinnati.

The Giants now hold the No. 5 and No. 10 picks in John Harbaugh’s first draft with the organization.

The last time they had two top-10 picks was in 2022, when they selected No. 5 and No. 7.
Dexter Lawrence is scheduled to travel to Cincinnati on Sunday for a physical that that would make the trade official. For now, the two sides have agreed to the trade, pending a physical.
Cincinnati believed that Dexter Lawrence would be more impactful this season, in a big year, than the 10th overall pick in this draft. Bengals didn’t wait to get aggressive.

Bengals defensive additions this offseason now include:

🏈Dexter Lawrence
🏈Boye Mafe
🏈Bryan Cook
🏈Jonathan Allen

Draft week blockbuster: The Giants are trading three-time Pro Bowl defensive tackle Dexter Lawrence to the Bengals in a deal that will include the 10th overall pick going to New York, per ESPN sources.

The Giants now head into Thursday night with the 5th and 10th overall picks.

Safe to say the @Giants did well with the return for Dexter Lawrence.

Updated picks ...

• Bengals: 2nd-rounder (41), 3rd-rounder (72), 4th-rounder (110), two 6th-rounders (189, 199), two 7th-rounders (221, 226).

• Giants: two 1st-rounders (5, 10), 2nd-rounder (37), 4th-rounder (105), 5th-rounder (145), three 6th-rounders (186, 192, 193).

Full terms of the Giants/Bengals deal ...

• Bengals get: DT Dexter Lawrence.

• Giants get: 10th overall pick.

No other picks involved. Cincinnati gets its guy. New York is now picking fifth and 10th overall on Thursday night.
